sourceMovie.title: Dawn of the Dead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dawn of the Dead works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Army of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Army of the Dead draws from the same horror well as Dawn of the Dead, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Extinction (2015) captures the horror appeal of Dawn of the Dead without simply imitating it. The action streak running through both films is what puts Extinction at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Zombieland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of Dawn of the Dead — that dark edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into Zombieland (2009). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Zombieland: Double Tap
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Zombieland: Double Tap (2019) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Dawn of the Dead rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Hills Have Eyes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Hills Have Eyes (2006) is the kind of film Dawn of the Dead fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: 28 Years Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 28 Years Later shares Dawn of the Dead's gift for horror — the 2025 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Dawn of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Dawn of the Dead compelling — the horror, the science-fiction — shows up in Dawn of the Dead (1978) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Return of the Living Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Dawn of the Dead is alive in The Return of the Living Dead (1985), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: We Were Soldiers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: We Were Soldiers (2002) operates in the same action as Dawn of the Dead, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: [REC]²
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: [REC]² (2009) belongs to the same conversation as Dawn of the Dead — both anchor their horror in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dawn of the Dead?
faq.answer: Try Army of the Dead, Extinction, Zombieland—each shares DNA with Dawn of the Dead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dawn of the Dead?
faq.answer: Dawn of the Dead (2004) is best described as Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dawn of the Dead worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
